# SC-03: Pre-Guardrails

**Track:** Security Controls (`SC`) · **Priority:** MUST (mandatory) · **Status:** draft

Screen all incoming change requests for prompt injection attacks and adversarial inputs that could manipulate agent interpretation of the requirements. Block manipulated inputs before they influence any downstream agent action.

## Required In Stages

| Stage | Name | Applicability |
|-------|------|---------------|
| 1 | Intent Ingestion | Mandatory |

## Delegation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Pattern** | Fully automated |
| **Agent does** | Scan incoming request for injection patterns, adversarial framing, and manipulation attempts. Block and log any detected attempts. |
| **Human does** | Review blocked request reports; investigate and resolve with requester. |

## Inputs

| Artifact | Source Control | Description |
|----------|---------------|-------------|
| Raw change request | Unprocessed incoming request before any agent parsing. |  |


## Outputs

| Artifact | Type | Description | Retention |
|----------|------|-------------|-----------|
| Guardrail scan result | evidence | Pass/fail result with details of any detected injection patterns. | 7 years (DORA) |


## Automation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Frequency** | per_change |
| **SLA** | — |
| **Tooling** | — |

## Failure Behaviour

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **On failure** | block |
| **Escalate to** | Security Architect |

Detected injection attempts block processing entirely. Attempt is logged and flagged to security team.

## Dependencies

None

## Regulatory Mapping

| Regulation | Article | Obligation |
|------------|---------|------------|
| DORA | Art. 9(2) | Protection of ICT systems from unauthorised access and manipulation |
| EU AI Act | Art. 15(4) | AI systems must be resilient against errors, faults, and manipulation attempts |


## Ownership

**Owner team:** Security · **Owner role:** Security Architect
